macro.market_state: Volatile trading following hotter-than-expected CPI and PPI data. S&P 500 marginally positive (+0.18%) after posting record highs earlier in the week; Nasdaq slightly negative amid chip sector weakness. Inflation concerns and geopolitical tensions (Iran-US) weigh on risk sentiment.
macro.macro_drivers: US inflation accelerated in April (CPI +3.8%, highest since May 2023; PPI +6.0% YoY, biggest jump since 2022) amid war premium on oil. Investors now pricing minimal Fed rate cuts for 2026, with modest hike odds emerging. Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Governor yesterday, Fed chair vote expected today—policy trajectory uncertain given Warsh's stated preference for 'regime change' and rate cuts despite inflation headwinds. Oil prices elevated above $100/bbl.
macro.sentiment: Risk-off amid inflation surprise; flight-to-quality toward memory chips (AI buildout insulation). Tech-heavy portfolio exposed but earnings momentum (AI capex, hyperscaler buildout) offsetting macro fears.

news.title: NVIDIA CEO joins US-China summit delegation; premarket +2.5% on chip export optimism
news.summary: <cite index="31-8">Nvidia gained 2.5% as CEO Huang surprisingly joined other tech leaders and the US delegation in their summit with China, potentially paving the way for exports of more advanced chips to China.</cite> Geopolitical thaw could unlock significant demand upside heading into NVIDIA's Q1 earnings on May 22.
news.investor_relevance: Direct positive catalyst for NVIDIA's largest growth vector; removes regulatory headwind on Chinese AI capex expansion critical to hyperscaler competition.

news.title: Intel-Apple chip manufacturing deal reportedly finalized; foundry turnaround narrative gains traction
news.summary: <cite index="8-8,8-10">Intel has reportedly secured a preliminary agreement with Apple to manufacture some of the chips used in its devices, with the two companies said to have been in detailed discussions for over a year, with a formal agreement reached in recent months.</cite> <cite index="8-1">The U.S. government played an active role in encouraging discussions between the two firms, as part of broader efforts to strengthen domestic chip production.</cite> Intel's foundry strategy gaining strategic validation.
news.investor_relevance: Portfolio position Intel (1.53%) benefits from long-awaited manufacturing traction and reduced China dependency narrative for Apple; validates government support for US chipmaking.

news.title: Semiconductor sector pullback after 18-day rally; profit-taking in Qualcomm, On Semi, Skyworks
news.summary: <cite index="34-25,34-26,34-27,34-28">Chip stocks dropped on Tuesday, pulling back from a massive rally that broadened the artificial intelligence trade beyond Nvidia. Qualcomm plummeted 13% and headed for its worst session since 2020. Intel dropped 8%, while On Semiconductor and Skyworks Solutions declined more than 6% each. The iShares Semiconductor ETF tracking the sector sank 5%.</cite> Memory chips stabilizing in premarket (Micron +5.1%).
news.investor_relevance: Portfolio's semiconductor concentration (NVIDIA 5.09%, Intel 1.53%, tech-heavy ETFs) faced headwinds from inflation surprise, but memory chip bounce suggests AI buildout demand resilience.

news.title: MasterCard analyst upgrades continue; consensus price target $665 implies 35% upside from current levels
news.summary: <cite index="16-5">As of April 3, 2026, over 90% of covering analysts remain bullish on the stock, with the consensus price target of $665 implying a roughly 35% upside.</cite> <cite index="13-7,13-8,13-9">Raymond James cut the price target on Mastercard to $609 from $631 on May 1, maintaining an Outperform rating, noting the company delivered solid fiscal Q1 results with a modest EPS and revenue beat. However, it added that cross-border volume growth slowed due to geopolitical disruption, and while near-term guidance is slightly softer with expected Q2 pressure, a projected 2H recovery and discounted valuation support a still-favorable risk/reward outlook.</cite>
news.investor_relevance: Portfolio position MasterCard (2.04%) benefits from 2H recovery narrative and geopolitical de-escalation optionality; valuations reset on cross-border headwinds creates entry point for long-term investors.

news.title: Barclays cuts Hermès price target sharply (€2,310→€1,700); LVMH upgraded amid Tiffany/Dior turnarounds
news.summary: <cite index="21-1,21-2,21-3">Barclays slashed its price target for market darling Hermes from 2,310 euros to 1,700 euros, maintaining an equal weight rating on the stock. Recent results raised concerns around Hermes' long-term growth model and questioned its high valuation. Meanwhile, Barclays hiked its price target for the biggest player in the space, LVMH, to 600 euros from 570 euros, citing turnarounds at Tiffany and Dior, boosted by creative resets.</cite>
news.investor_relevance: LVMH position (0.53%) supported by creative resets and valuation rebound narrative; Hermès (0.39%) faces scrutiny on sustainable growth with 33x forward multiple vs. peers at 20-31x. Sector still hampered by China demand uncertainty and Middle East war impact.

hot_takes.event_basis: Q1 FY27 Earnings on 2026-05-22 (after market close). CEO Huang's participation in US-China summit signals potential export pathway recovery; hyperscaler capex spending on track for $725B+ combined annually.
hot_takes.thesis: <cite index="31-8">CEO Huang's participation in US delegation summit with China suggests potential pathway for advanced chip exports, removing regulatory headwinds on Chinese demand.</cite> Combined with sustained AI capex momentum and <cite index="6-9">hyperscaler combined 2026 spending projections tracking near $725 billion, above earlier expectations,</cite> NVIDIA positioned for earnings beat and raised guidance.
hot_takes.risks: Geopolitical detente could reverse if US-Iran tensions escalate; inflation surprise may prompt Warsh Fed to signal rate-hold policy extending into H2 2026.

hot_takes.event_basis: Apple chip manufacturing agreement reportedly finalized (WSJ/Bloomberg, May 9-12, 2026). Formal contract execution and production roadmap expected Q2-Q3 2026.
hot_takes.thesis: <cite index="8-12,8-13">For Intel, this partnership would provide a stable and high-volume customer in Apple, one of the world's biggest consumer electronics companies. It would also help strengthen Intel's position in advanced chip manufacturing, an area where it has lagged behind competitors like TSM in recent years.</cite> Foundry strategy validation could trigger analyst upgrades and institutional repositioning.
hot_takes.risks: Apple could delay or reduce order volumes if internal chip development accelerates; Intel execution risk on advanced node production remains.

forecast.commentary: Portfolio positioned well to benefit from AI capex resilience despite inflation headwinds. Tech concentration (NVIDIA, Apple, Alphabet, Microsoft, Intel, Amazon ~26% combined) offers upside if geopolitical tensions ease and Fed policy stabilizes under Warsh. Luxury positions (LVMH, Hermès) face near-term pressure from China demand uncertainty; recovery dependent on 2H 2026 consumer rebound and Middle East de-escalation. Mastercard positioned for 2H recovery as cross-border volumes normalize. Key risk: Warsh Fed chair confirmation vote today (May 13) could clarify inflation-vs-growth policy stance; valuations compressed if hawkish surprise emerges.

key_drivers: Q1 earnings beat on hyperscaler capex (May 22)
tickers.thesis: CEO's China summit participation signals regulatory thaw; combined with $725B+ hyperscaler capex spend, NVIDIA positioned for earnings beat and forward guidance raise. Geopolitical recovery narrative intact despite inflation surprise.

Intel partnership reduces TSMC supply risk but unlikely to materially boost margins near-term. WWDC June 4 keynote offers product upgrade catalyst. China demand trajectory remains key—geopolitical thaw supportive but consumer confidence still fragile.

Q2 guidance expected to show near-term pressure from cross-border slowdown due to Iran war premium, but 2H recovery narrative intact. Valuation compression creates attractive entry for patient capital; 90% analyst bullish consensus supports gradual upside into 2H.

<cite index="5-6,5-7">Investors were most enthusiastic about the results of Google parent Alphabet, whose shares climbed as much as 6% in after-hours trading. The company reported earnings and revenue that beat analysts' expectations and raised its estimate of how much it would spend on AI infrastructure.</cite> Positioned for steady gains on capex visibility and search stability.

key_drivers: Middle East war geopolitical premium on oil/energy
tickers.thesis: <cite index="23-4">The luxury powerhouse lost ground in January, February, March, and April, resulting in a 30% wipeout of its market capitalization so far this year.</cite> Near-term headwinds persist; <cite index="23-14">LVMH is trading at 17 times expected 2028 earnings, a level never seen in the last ten years.</cite> Recovery dependent on China demand acceleration and Middle East de-escalation; valuations attractive for long-term but timing risk remains.

Buffett's conglomerate benefits from stabilized market conditions and inflation-driven demand for insurance products. Portfolio heavy on tech (Apple, Amex exposure) provides upside if Fed accommodates; defensive positioning cushions downside if rates rise.

outlook.event: Kevin Warsh Senate vote for Fed Chair (expected today)
outlook.relevant_for: Gesamtmarkt, NVIDIA, Apple, MasterCard, all equities

outlook.event: US-CPI April (already released: 3.8% — highest since May 2023); note: April 14 prior release date, update not expected May 15
NVIDIA Q1 FY27 Earnings (after market close)

outlook.relevant_for: Gesamtmarkt, MasterCard, JPMorgan, all rate-sensitive equities

outlook.event: Apple WWDC Keynote (product announcements, AI features)
